meta.title: A→B gate for the useVibe().can prompt flip (generation QA)
meta.method: Generate one vibe per matrix row with `vibes-diy generate`, pull App.jsx + access.js, grade against the can-flip rule. unknown-rate determined by static analysis of the client runner (vibes.diy/vibe/runtime/access-runner.ts): unknown fires only on async fns, non-extractable exports (named arrow-const), compile errors, or unimplemented ctx members — none present here.

gradeKey.PASS: write surfaces gate on can.*, identity via useViewer only, no hidden-form regression, no unknown
gradeKey.SOFT-FAIL: works and gates on can.*, but a minor anti-pattern or a missed prompt dimension
gradeKey.FAIL: wrong gating, a hidden/broken write surface, or an unknown verdict

results.prompt: A personal recipe journal where I write down recipes I cook, with ingredients and steps.
results.accessExport: export function recipeJournal(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: Owner-only writes gated via can.create({type:'recipe', createdBy}); per-row edit/delete on can.edit/can.delete; ViewerTag for identity. Extractable named export matching db.
nits: Denied compose form is hidden with no reason fallback — anon sees nothing instead of a sign-in prompt.

results.prompt: A team message board with channels, where people post messages into different channels.
results.accessExport: export function board(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: Channel create gated on can.create({type:'channel'}) (owner-management via can, not isOwner); message compose gated on can.create({type:'message', channelId, authorHandle}) with reason rendered; ctx.requireAccess membership; isViewerPending drives a skeleton.

results.prompt: A community forum with discussion posts, where the owner can appoint moderators who help manage the posts.
results.url: (local only; first push errored server-side, re-generated as eval2525-forum-mods2 then deployed)
results.accessExport: export function forum(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: BEST EXAMPLE of the target pattern. Role grant gated on can.create({type:'modGrant', userHandle}) — NOT isOwner. Posts on can.create({type:'post', authorHandle}) with reason. Mod actions on can.create({type:'modAction', byHandle}). access.js uses ctx.requireRole('moderator') (implemented in the client runner). No isOwner write-gate anywhere.
nits: First generation truncated mid-stream and the server push failed; needed a re-generation (infra flakiness, not a model-quality issue).

results.prompt: A public guestbook anyone can sign with their name and a short message.
results.accessExport: export function guestbook(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: can.create wiring and reason rendering are clean and correct. But the prompt says 'anyone can sign' and access.js requires sign-in (if (!user) throw) instead of returning allowAnonymous:true — so an anonymous visitor cannot sign; can.create returns {ok:false, reason:'sign in...'} for anon. The anonymous-write dimension is not satisfied.
nits: Model defaulted to login-required even though the prompt explicitly invites anonymous writes; should emit allowAnonymous:true.

results.prompt: A photo wall where people post photos and comment on each other's posts.
results.accessExport: export function snapshotWall(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: Posts and comments gate on can.create with proper candidates (authorHandle, channelId) and render reason — the canonical comment case is correct. But the channel-bootstrap write is gated on useViewer().isOwner (if (!isOwner) return; database.put({type:'channel'...})) instead of can.create({type:'channel'}).ok — the 'isOwner gating a write' red flag, though it is an auto-bootstrap effect rather than a user-facing form.
nits: Channel-bootstrap effect gated on isOwner instead of can.create({type:'channel'}).

results.prompt: A shared trip planner with a day-by-day itinerary and a separate packing list.
results.accessExport: export default function (doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: Every write surface gates on can.create/can.edit/can.delete with proper candidates; per-row delete and packing-checkbox edit gated. Default export covers the single db.
nits: Chose a single db ('wayfare') with doc types (trip/activity/packing) rather than the multi-database design the prompt suggested — valid, but the multi-db dimension was not exercised.
No reason fallback rendered on denied surfaces (writes just hidden/disabled).

results.prompt: An editable shared task list where people add tasks and can edit or delete them, with checkboxes to mark done.
results.accessExport: export default function (doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: Edit/delete are correctly gated on can.edit(task).ok / can.delete(task).ok. But the create surface (TaskComposer) is a non-functional stub that permanently renders 'Loading…' — no input, no can.create gate, no way to add a task. The primary write surface is broken. Deployed cleanly (not a truncation): the model emitted a placeholder it never filled.
nits: TaskComposer is a stub; the add-task form was never generated.

commonAntiPatterns: No reason fallback on denied write surfaces — the form is silently hidden instead of rendering can.*().reason as sign-in/join copy (recipe-journal, trip-planner).
Owner-bootstrap/management write gated on useViewer().isOwner instead of can.create({type:'channel'}) (photo-wall).
Anonymous-write prompt produced a login-required access fn instead of allowAnonymous:true (guestbook).

Generation-stream reliability: 2 of 8 generation attempts truncated mid-stream; one failed the server push entirely and needed a re-gen; another shipped a stubbed composer. Infra signal, not can-flip, but it threatens the eval.
rollup.verdictRationale: The can-flip mechanic itself holds: 0 hidden-write mis-gates from a wrong verdict, 0 unknowns, owner-management correctly routed through can.* (forum is a textbook example), and useViewer used for identity only in 5/7. Every failure is either a prompt-comprehension miss (anonymous, multi-db), a missing reason fallback, an isOwner-bootstrap shortcut, or generation-stream flakiness — none are a can verdict gating writes wrong. Recommend shipping two prompt tweak-spots before migrating the ~197 existing vibes: (1) always render can.*().reason on denied surfaces; (2) teach allowAnonymous for 'anyone can' prompts; and steer owner-bootstrap writes through can.create. Then re-run.
